Information includes, but is not limited to, organization name, Employer Identification Number (EIN), reason for refund, staff requesting refund, staff approving refund, dates documenting original request, original payment, when request sent to Attorney General''s Office finance section, and check mailed.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years.

series_description: Helps staff inform media/Attorney General on topics such as school funding. Records contain, but are not limited to, a brief summary of the interview or event, informational narratives, statistics, and some past press releases or images of the interviewer.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years, prior to destroying have reviewed by State . ; Paper: Retain 4 years, unless scanned and saved electronically, prior to destroying have reviewed by State Archives.

series_description: Materials and information related to presentations, trainings, and workshops developed and/or conducted by AGO staff concerning issues of security compliance to state and federal regulations, such as protecting "Federal Tax Information (FTI)", "Criminal Justice Information (CJI)", or information protected by the "Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (HIPAA)". Records include, but are not limited to, presentations, handouts, reference materials used to develop trainings, logistical arrangements, correspondence, copies of contracts, agreements and/or payment information, evaluations, registration forms, and attendance documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 6 years, provided audited. ; Paper: Retain 6 years, provided audited, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents review or modification of a license, permit, certification, or registration through administrative hearing process. Records include, but are not limited to, notice of opportunity for hearing, request for hearing, scheduling information, continuances, file sent by client on respondent (gathered during investigative of respondent), exhibits, correspondence, attorney work product, report and recommendation from hearing officer, and final adjudication order.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 7 years from case closing. ; Paper: Retain 7 years from case closing, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents initial notice of a bankruptcy declaration by an individual or company by the Ohio Attorney General (AGO). AGO will inform client agencies whom may have interest in pursuing legal action. Records include, but are not limited to, notices from bankruptcy court or client agency, correspondence, and responses from client agencies regarding whether or not action should be pursued. If the AGO pursues legal action, the records will be retained per the applicable Environmental section''s case files retention schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 8 years. If legal action pursued, retain per applicable Environmental section''s case file retention schedule. ; Paper: Retain 8 year, unless scanned and saved electronically. If legal action pursued, retain per applicable Environmental section''s case file retention schedule.

series_description: Documents the collection of payments from debtors owing money to a client agency of the Environmental Protection section based on a consent order or judgment. Records include, but are not limited to, ledger of funds owed/balances, payment schedules, checks received, deposit slips, payment demand letters, and satisfaction of judgment letters.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until consent order or judgment is terminated, provided audited. ; Paper: Retain until consent order or judgment is terminated, provided audited, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents enforcement actions regarding violations within the charitable sector. Records include, but are not limited to, copies of enforcement action records including settlement agreements, assurances of discontinuance, and judgments.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain permanently, unless microfilmed. ; Paper: If in paper, retain permanently, unless microfilmed. ; Microform: If microfilmed, retain permanently.
disposition: Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred ; Microform: Permanent

series_description: Documents enforcement and non-enforcement litigation including, but not limited to, legal action against environmental violations, breaches of contract, civil rights disputes, and negligence cases brought against the state. Joint Federal Enforcement Actions fall within this series as well. Records include, but are not limited to, complaints, correspondence, referral documents, investigative material, research, pleadings, memorandum, affidavits, motions, judgments, discovery, evidence, transcripts, attorney/work product, environmental analyses, settlements and negotiation documents, hearing and deposition materials, and consent orders/decrees. May possibly include ledger of funds owed/balances, payment schedules, checks received, deposit slips, payment demand letters, and satisfaction of judgment letters.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 10 years from case closing or 1 year if consent order is terminated ; Paper: Retain 10 years from case closing or 1 year if consent order is terminated, unless scanned and saved electronically

series_description: Documents request for Ohio Attorney General (AGO)-held data from external government agencies and other entities. Records include, but are not limited to, copy of memorandum of understanding or contract, correspondence, any data provided by requestor used to identify data to extract, extract and/or processed/redacted request files, and applicable consent and/or legal compliance forms.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years after certificate of is received. ; Paper: Retain 2 years after certificate of destruction is received, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents the destruction of Ohio Attorney General (AGO)-owned data outputs or extracts received by requesting government agencies or other entities from the AGO per the terms of an memorandum of understanding or contract. Records include information on, but is not limited to, description of information destroyed, date of destruction, description of destruction method, individuals and witnesses involved in the destruction, as well as information regarding vendors used in destruction process.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain permanently, unless printed or microfilmed. ; Paper: If in paper, retain permanently, unless microfilmed. ; Microform: If microfilmed, retain permanently.
disposition: Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred ; Microform: Permanent

series_description: Correspondence and/or related documents received from government agencies, businesses or the general public pertaining to data breaches of computerized systems containing personal information and potential disclosure of personal information contained in those systems. Information may be used for investigative or civil action by the Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO) per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 year. ; Paper: Retain 1 year, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Recordings of in-bound and out-bound calls for quality assurance, security assessment, or for a Collections-related matter(s). [Any call needed for investigation, litigation, or for a Collections-related matter(s) will be copied from the system and retained according to the appropriate retention schedule for the investigation or case to which it pertains.]
retention_statement: Electronic: Records automatically deleted after 90 days based on third-party service''s recording systems. [Any call needed for investigation, litigation, or for a Collections-related matter(s) will be copied from the system and retained according to the appropriate retention schedule for the investigation or case to which it pertains.]

series_title: Attorney General Joint Committee on Agency Rule Review (JCARR) Rulemaking Documents
series_description: Documents the Ohio Attorney General''s (AGO) adoption, amendment, and rescission of Ohio Administrative Code (OAC) rules as set forth in
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 6 years. ; Paper: Retain 6 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_title: Outside Audit Reports and Office Responses
series_description: Documents audit findings issued by external entities like the Auditor of State and Federal Government regarding Ohio Attorney General (AGO) operations. Records include, but are not limited to, audit report summarizing findings and recommendations for corrective action as well as responses by the AGO on what their corrective action plans are.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 10 years. ; Paper: Retain 10 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: System test data is pulled from Ohio Attorney General (AGO) production systems, manufactured by the AGO, or through user tests to be used as testing material for new hardware or software system features, database updates or modifications, or for reporting needs to determine if desired output is occurring. Data is chosen randomly for some instances or specifically for other instances, such as tests on a specific field where information is entered in (i.e. Social Security Numbers); data is typically obfuscated.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until no longer useful for the system it is related to.

series_description: Outward-facing communications to the public to educate or relay information concerning the work/services of the Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO). Records include, but are not limited to, emails, draft scripts, and podcast recordings.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years, prior to destroying have reviewed by State .

series_description: Documents vendor performance for possible future reference by sections of the office for seeking purchase of goods or services. Records include, but are not limited to, vendor performance review form and possible vendor response.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 fiscal years.

series_description: Temporary files received from outside record-keeping entities that are then loaded and saved on AGO systems or intermediary files sent from AGO office in order to complete a file/data transmission. Records include, but are not limited to, copies of received or sent documents or data being saved to or pulled from AGO systems.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 6 months after loaded in or sent from AGO systems.

series_title: State Awards for Economic Development Reviews
series_description: Per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years from release of final report. Destroy once requirements of ORC 149.11 are completed. ; Paper: Retain 2 years from release of final report, unless scanned and saved electronically. Destroy once requirements of ORC 149.11 are completed.

series_description: Documents submission of calculated indirect cost plans for Federal government review in order to allow use of grant funding for agency programs or billing client agencies for attorney general work. Records include, but are not limited to, expense reports for review, organizational charts, indirect cost plan, and approval/denial from Federal government for use of funds.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years, provided audited.

series_description: Records concerning settlement funds held outside of State Treasury Fund by Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO) and any relating transactions concerning these funds per
retention_statement: Electronic: Until settlement fund depleted, provided audited.

series_description: Per Ohio Revised Code 124.14, documents process to assign employees to job classifications that fit their positions as well as document changes or job audits of classification specifications, position descriptions, and other designations. The final determination from job audit added to employee''s personnel file. The final version of classification specification would be filed with the Ohio Department of Administrative Services. Records include, but are not limited to, input gathered from sections of the office, unions, or employees; allocation chart showing pay ranges; drafts and edits to job classifications; reports; and salary information and analysis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after completion. ; Paper: Retain 5 years after completion, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Legal advice or counsel, issued under the signature of the Attorney General, based on questions of law posed to the office, generally in regards to how a particular statute should be read, applied or interpreted. Records can include, but are not limited to, the request, acknowledgement, response, correspondence, and other pertinent material relating to the request and response.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 7 years. ; Paper: Retain 7 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Case files serve as primary source of litigation information for case filed in state and federal courts concerning Ohio Attorney General (AGO) client agencies. Records include, but are not limited to, pleadings, memorandum, affidavits, attorney work product, legal research, motions, judgements, discovery, evidence, transcripts, correspondence, settlement and negotiation documents, and hearing and deposition materials.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after case has closed and all appeals have exhausted. ; Paper: Retain 5 years after case has closed and all appeals have exhausted, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_title: Outside Counsel Firm Files and Retention Agreements
series_description: Files compiled and used to make decisions on hiring outside counsel firms. Those hired will contain a contract between the Ohio Attorney General's Office (AGO) and the outside counsel that establishes the provisions of legal services to be provided by the outside counsel, as well as reporting responsibilities, salary and budget information, and other terms of the agreement. Records include, but are not limited to, completed request for qualifications (RFQ), firm biographies, affidavit of eligibility, declaration of material assistance (DMA), affirmative action plan verification (AAPV), W-9 and vendor ID forms, conflict waivers, retention agreement, correspondence, and quarterly status reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 6 years from end of fiscal year. ; Paper: Retain 6 years from end of fiscal year. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately.

series_title: Outside Counsel Proposals to Attorney General
series_description: Outside Counsel firms proposed cases to the Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO). Records include, but are not limited to, correspondence, spreadsheet data from pension funds, and memos from law firms assessing strengths of the potential cases.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 6 months. ; Paper: Retain 6 months, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_title: Crime Victims Compensation Award Application Files
series_description: Documents application, review, and possible awarding of crime victim claims for supplemental assistance from the Ohio Victims of Crime Compensation Program per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 7 years after either award is satisfied, the application is denied, no more supplemental reparations can be awarded, or the award has reached maximum amount and all appeals have exhausted for the claim. ; Paper: Retain 7 years after either award is satisfied, the application is denied, no more supplemental reparations can be awarded, or the award has reached maximum amount and all appeals have exhausted for the claim. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately.

series_description: Records tracking debts owed and payment history to client agencies collected by the Ohio Attorney General's Office per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain up to 40 years unless otherwise specified by rule or client agreement, or 10 years if debt is satisfied, discharged, or canceled; whichever occurs first. ; Paper: Retain up to 40 years unless otherwise specified by rule or client agreement, or 10 years if debt is satisfied, discharged, or canceled; whichever occurs first. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately.

series_description: Files maintained on attorneys who have applied to serve as special counsel to the Ohio Attorney General''s Office''s Collections Enforcement section. Records include, but are not limited to, copies of signed retention agreements, Bureau of Workers Compensation insurance certificate, appointment letter, activity tracking reports and correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 8 years from expiration of appointment, unless scanned and saved electronically. ; Electronic: Retain 8 years from expiration of appointment.

series_title: Unable to Locate (UTL) Payments and Refunds
series_description: Payments or refund requests received by the Ohio Attorney General''s Office''s Collections Enforcement section, but are unable to be connected to an existing debtor account file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years, unless scanned and saved electronically. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years.

series_description: Account list reports generated daily to summarize day's work (transactions, certifications, adjustments, etc.), errors that have occurred within the file transfer process, listing of accounts, system activity, and other reference purposes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years.

series_title: Corrections and Habeas Corpus Litigation Files
series_description: Corrections and Habeas Corpus cases are brought in state and federal courts. Case files track the work of the office to oppose litigation brought by inmates seeking to oppose prison conditions (Corrections) or prison sentences (Habeas Corpus). Records include, but may not be limited to, pleadings, exhibits, court orders, decisions, petition, case-related correspondence, discovery, and deposition transcripts.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years from case closing. ; Paper: 5 years from case closing, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents requests by eligible inmates for post-conviction DNA testing per Ohio Revised Code (ORC) 2953.71 and 2953.81. The Ohio Attorney General''s Office receives a copy of the request per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 90 days after decision is made. ; Paper: Retain 90 days after decision is made, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Press releases regarding news about office initiatives and litigation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years, prior to destroying have reviewed by State . ; Paper: Retain 4 years, unless scanned and saved electronically, prior to destroying have reviewed by State Archives.

series_title: Columns Written by the Attorney General
series_description: Columns that discuss the Attorney General''s opinion on major issues and office initiatives.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years, prior to destroying have reviewed by State . ; Paper: Retain 4 years, unless scanned and saved electronically, prior to destroying have reviewed by State Archives.

series_description: Internal information about office issues that staff uses to field media questions and prepare the Attorney General for interviews.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years. ; Paper: Retain 2 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Briefings used to prepare the Attorney General for the next day''s media interview or travel day.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years. ; Paper: Retain 2 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Articles from newspapers and websites about the Attorney General and other office issues to have on file for reference.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years. ; Paper: Retain 2 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Newsletters sent or posted promoting issues and initiatives from various sections of the Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO). Records include, but are not limited to, mass mailing emails and postings on the AGO website.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years. ; Paper: Retain 4 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Informational or promotional videos produced by the Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO) for press releases, social media, and other initiatives of the office.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years, prior to destroying have reviewed by State .

series_description: Documents the Ohio Attorney General''s solicitation to law schools for potential recruitment of recent graduates. Records include, but are not limited to, cover letter, resume, transcript, writing sample, offer letter or close out letter, rater sheets, and interview questions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years from date of offer letter or close out letter. ; Paper: Retain 2 years from date of offer letter or close out letter, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Solicited and unsolicited resumes from applicants interested in a position at the Ohio Attorney General''s Office. Records include, but are not limited to, resumes, applications, correspondence, and letters of reference. These records do not pertain to a specific job posting.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years. ; Paper: Retain 2 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents the hiring process for those applying to Ohio Attorney General position openings. Records include, but are not limited to, interview rater sheets, resumes and/or applications, posting descriptions, recommendation to hire forms, professional references, correspondence, and other applicable information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years after position filled or closed. ; Paper: Retain 2 years after position filled or closed, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents pertaining to the partnership non-paid volunteers (externs), which demonstrate proof of partnership. Records include, but are not limited to, proof of enrollment, Ohio civil service application and/or resume, externship agreement, policy acknowledgments, and paperwork required by school (if applicable), and correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 year from start date. ; Paper: Retain 1 year from start date, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents review and confirmation of a past or current employment of an AGO employee for outside entities such as financial institutions or state bar associations. The AGO may confirm employment information such as dates of employment, position(s) held, and salary.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years from answering. ; Paper: Retain 2 years from answering, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Records concerning allegations of non-Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) workplace violations such as workplace harassment, policy violations and misconduct, and other non-discriminatory complaints and/or investigations. Such investigations may use Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) and/or other investigator resources. Records include, but are not limited to, complaints, notes, statements, correspondence, investigatory reports, interviews, determinations, record of actions taken.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years from close of investigation. ; Paper: Retain 3 years from close of investigation, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: As mandated by the Immigration Reform and Control Act of 1986, the United State Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) I-9 form is used to verify an employee''s identity and legal authorization to work within the United States. The form contains information such as the employee''s name, social security number, date of birth, and employer review and verification of documented identification of the employee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years from date of hiring or 1 year after employment is terminated, whichever is later.

series_description: Communications to building management requesting non-standard access to Ohio Attorney General buildings/floors.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 year, unless printed. ; Paper: Retain 1 year.

series_description: Per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years. ; Paper: Retain 5 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years. ; Paper: Retain 3 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Originals and copies of records collected from external providers, informants, other agencies or records created internally by the Health Care Fraud section. Records include, but are not limited to, video surveillance recordings, interview recordings, medical records, and banking records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Transfer originals to owner as mandated by statute or court order. If evidence is not claimed but consists of copies of original documents, which are maintained by the original source, destroy. If the evidence consists of originals and is either not claimed or was created internally, retain 4 years from case closing. If evidence was created internally and a copy of materials is already being retained as part of a case file, destroy. ; Paper: Transfer originals to owner as mandated by statute or court order. If evidence is not claimed but consists of copies of original documents, which are maintained by the original source, destroy. If the evidence consists of originals and is either not claimed or was created internally, retain 4 years from case closing. If evidence was created internally and a copy of materials is already being retained as part of a case file, destroy.

series_description: Ohio Organized Crime Investigation Commission (OOCIC) rules and regulations to operate within.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until revised, superseded, obsolete or no longer of administrative value. ; Paper: Retain until revised, superseded, obsolete or no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Files concerning fleet vehicles owned by the Ohio Organized Crime Investigations Commission (OOCIC). Records include, but are not limited to, service records, operation maintenance reports of vehicle, warranties and guaranty statements, operating manual, accident reports, repair records, and salvage information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 years after vehicle is salvaged or sold. ; Paper: Retain 1 years after vehicle is salvaged or sold, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Tracks location, custody, and status of equipment used by the Ohio Organized Crime Investigations Commission (OOCIC). Records concern, but are not limited to, equipment like GPS units, vehicles, cameras, investigative equipment, walkie-talkie radios, computers, and cellular phones.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years after equipment salvaged, destroyed, or transferred ownership, provided audited. ; Paper: Retain 3 years after equipment salvaged, destroyed, or transferred ownership, provided audited, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_title: Lease Records of Lands or Buildings
series_description: Records documenting leases obtained by the Ohio Organized Crime Investigations Commission (OOCIC) for task force and investigative work.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years after expiration of lease. ; Paper: Retain 2 years after expiration of lease, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents complaints or queries submitted by civilians or local law enforcement to the Ohio Organized Crime Investigations Commission (OOCIC) concerning criminal activity. Complaints are often used to determine if an investigative task force should be started. Records include, but are not limited to, a civilian complaint form, correspondence with local law enforcement, related or responsive documentation provided by the complainant, and any responses made by the OOCIC.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years after closure or rejection. ; Paper: Retain 3 years after closure or rejection, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents equipment and service requests from covert task forces. Such needs include, but are not limited to, equipment needed, equipment or building repairs, and computer support.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 years after completion of work. ; Paper: Retain 1 years after completion of work, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents relating to the application and grant or denial of an administrative license or permit to be used during the course of an Ohio Organized Crime Investigations Commission (OOCIC) task force operation. Such licenses or permits could concern radio or x-ray operator licenses.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 year after final expiration, renewal, or denial. ; Paper: Retain 1 year after final expiration, renewal, or denial, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents financial transactions and use of commission funds relating to task force operations. Records include, but are not limited to, bank statements, invoices, vouchers, travel expense reports, and other expenditure documents created during a task force''s operation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after closing of task force, provided audited. ; Paper: Retain 5 years after closing of task force, provided audited, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Tables of organization for each section of the Ohio Attorney General''s Office listing employee name, classification or working title, and position number.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced. ; Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced.

series_title: Compulsory Process Materials (Other Federal or State Law Enforcement or Regulatory Agencies)
series_description: Discovery materials (compulsory process materials) in which the compulsory process originated a governmental agency other than the Ohio Attorney General’s Office (AGO). Such materials are received from, but not limited to, Federal law enforcement agencies, law enforcement agencies of other states, or Ohio law enforcement or regulatory agencies other than the AGO.
retention_statement: Electronic: Such period as consented to by the producing party or as ordered by statute or courts in jurisdictions outside of the AGO. ; Paper: Such period as consented to by the producing party or as ordered by statute or courts in jurisdictions outside of the AGO.

series_description: Documents outlining internal policies, procedures, and/or protocols used by sections of the Ohio Attorney General''s Office. Used to inform employees of section practices and operational standards.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, replaced, or made obsolete.

series_description: Documents used to train and/or provide information about the status of the office as it transitions from one administration to the next. Reports include, but are not limited to, summarizing section functions, organizational charts/staffing structure, assignments/responsibilities, upcoming decisions or key events, pending matters, internal procedures, and other information needed to inform.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 9 years after applicable transition. ; Paper: Retain 9 years after applicable transition, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Research and advice provided by the Opinions Section to senior administration and other section of the Ohio Attorney General''s Office (AGO) regarding to matters unrelated to the preparation of formal opinions. Records include, but are not limited to, advice, results of research, and memorandum.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 10 years. ; Paper: Retain 10 years. Dispose of if scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Written guidance, counsel, or information based on questions of law posed. Unlike a Formal Opinion, informal opinions concern issues that may be considered privileged attorney/client communication. Records include, but are not limited to, the initial request, response from section, advisory letters and memoranda, correspondence, and attachments or pertinent references included with the response.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 7 years. ; Paper: Retain 7 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Documents work orders handled by the Information Technologies Client Services for the AGO and some constituents like law enforcement agencies, courts, and some instances the public. Entries contain information about the requester, the work order, the technician assigned to the order, the related AGO section, the location the order concerns (if applicable), the date the order was created, the date the order was closed.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 7 years after the work order completion. If work order(s) concern an Information Technologies project, that work order(s) will be retained per the applicable "Project Management Office Files" retention schedule.

series_description: Files that document the life-cycle of an Information Technology Services (ITS) project from the initial work order requesting the development, the designing, and final approval and initiation of a new system. ITS Project Management, Infrastructure, and Security units will work with AG sections in the development of these projects. Once a project is completed, the system will become operational and managed by the Infrastructure unit. Records can include, but are not limited to, work orders and enhancement work orders, design documents, schedules, system test plans, and sign-offs. Data and test data housed in resulting systems will not fall under this schedule, but under retention schedules specific to those records'' information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years after life of system/related system(s) plus needed time to convert/migrate data to newer system or if project is canceled. For systems to be decommissioned, retain with the longest applicable records retention schedule associated with the data housed within the system. ; Paper: Retain 2 years after life of system/related system(s) plus needed time to convert/migrate data to newer system or if project is canceled, unless scanned and saved electronically. For systems to be decommissioned, retain with the longest applicable records retention schedule associated with the data housed within the system, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: Records created and gathered necessary to take actions to enforce Ohio consumers laws. Records include, but may not be limited to, complaints; correspondence; transactional documents; witness and/or evidence databases; communication with other agencies, law enforcement, or companies or persons being investigated; pre-suit subpoenas and responses; research; cease and desist orders; requests for substantiation of claims and responses; assurances of voluntary compliance; pleadings and orders; evidentiary materials; expert witness materials; discovery materials; trial preparation material; attorney work product; copies of financial records transcripts; and post-judgment compliance records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after investigation or case closed. ; Paper: Retain 5 years after investigation or case closed, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_description: File used to monitor violations of the Ohio Consumer Sales Practices Act (CSPA). Records include, but are not limited to, notice from county clerks of court, per
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years from date of receipt by AGO, unless scanned and saved electronically. ; Electronic: 5 years from date of receipt by AGO.

series_description: Records containing court decisions (judgments) involving consumer law cases in which the Ohio courts have ruled a specific act or practice violates
retention_statement: Electronic: Permanent, unless in paper or microfilmed. ; Paper: If in paper, retain permanently, unless microfilmed. ; Microform: If microfilmed, retain permanently.
disposition: Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred ; Microform: Permanent

series_description: Complaint records compiled in order to work out informal agreements between a supplier or service provider and the complainant. Details on a complaint are logged in the complaint management system of the office. Records include, but are not limited to, initial complaint forms; correspondences to and from the complainant, supplier, and Ohio Attorney General’s Office; and documents relating to the transaction (copies of contracts, receipts, pictures, estimates, warranties, bills of lading, advertisements, brochures, photographs, etc.); and the electronic details maintained in the complaint management system. Records concerning unwanted call notification database records, identity theft complaints, and Title Defect Recision Fund files will fall under their own respective retention schedules.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from receipt of complaint. ; Paper: Retain 5 years from receipt of complaint. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately. Material received from complainant will be returned if not needed.

series_description: Per ORC 2981, evidence collected from local law enforcement, victims, or subpoenaed material to be used in Consumer Protection investigations, some of which may be confidential in nature. Records concerning evidence control forms, chain of custody, and receipts of returned evidence will be retained separately and with the investigative case file.
retention_statement: Electronic: Following the expiration of statute of limitations without filing charges or following the expiration of time for all appeals: (1) return property to persons entitled to possession per ORC 2981.06(E) or (2) follow disposal for property per ORC 2981.12. ; Paper: Following the expiration of statute of limitations without filing charges or following the expiration of time for all appeals: (1) return property to persons entitled to possession per ORC 2981.06(E) or (2) follow disposal for property per ORC 2981.12.

series_title: Property Room Evidence Log/Evidence Control Records
series_description: Records used to track the date received, chain of custody, and final disposition of evidence collected by the Consumer Protection section for criminal investigation and/or civil case work.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from date of disposition. ; Paper: Retain 5 years from date of disposition.

series_description: Opinions written any time that a piece of real property is sold or purchased by the State of Ohio which serve to determine who holds claims to a property in the State of Ohio and assures that the title is clear of all liens and previous claims. Includes listing of encumbrances on the property and summary of what is not assured by the opinion.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years. ; Paper: Retain 5 years, unless scanned and saved electronically.

series_title: Criminal Justice Information System (CJIS) Audit Logs
series_description: Logs that track the auditing capabilities of an information system holding CJIS-related information such as login records, permission and password changes, administrator commands, modifications or deletion of information, and remote network access; identify audit-related information that establish what type of events occurred, when the event occurred, where the event occurred, the source of the event, the outcome of the event, and identification of those associated with the event that occurred within the system; and retains information that will help reconstruct events in the event of unauthorized activity or malfunctions within the information system.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 8 years.

series_description: Records received by the Ohio Attorney General's Office (AGO) in which complainants disclose information related to unwanted calls. Details on a complaint are logged in Unwanted Call Notification Database for review by the AGO for possible investigation. Records include, but are not limited to, initial complaint forms. These records are unrelated to Consumer Dispute Resolution Records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after receiving. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years after receiving.

series_description: Per
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years from receiving in the database. ; Paper: Retain 4 years from receiving. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately.

series_description: Tips and referrals received by the Ohio Attorney General's Office (AGO) concerning possible human trafficking activity. After review, information may be forwarded to local Human Trafficking task forces, local law enforcement, or the Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) for possible investigation. Records include, but are not limited to, recording of calls, call records logged in call system, Human Trafficking Hotline forms, text and email messages, web tip forms, and application push notifications and media communications.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from receiving. If tip/referral is forwarded to BCI to investigate, records will be retained per applicable investigative records retention schedule. ; Paper: Retain 5 years from receiving. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately. If tip/referral is forwarded to BCI to investigate, records will be retained per applicable investigative records retention schedule.

series_description: Evidence submitted to the Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) by a law enforcement agency, prosecutor, subject, or witness, to be forensically analyzed. BCI provides forensic services on the evidence, provides written and automated forensic reports regarding the evidence, and returns the original evidence to the original agency afterwards. Records include, but are not limited to, evidence received from law enforcement agency, prosecutor, or witness; imaged or extract files of the evidence for BCI to work with; intake forms; technical reports generated by forensic tools; written narrative reports generated by BCI staff; and resulting forensic copies of the evidence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years from case closing by BCI. Analysis and narrative report will be retained by BCI per the applicable investigative records retention schedule.

series_title: Automated Program Listing and Source Code
series_description: Automated program code which generates the machine-language instructions used to operate an automated information system.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years from last use, modification, or decommissioning of the associated system or application related to the code.

series_description: Records documenting the rejection of out-of-state peace officers, corrections officers, or security officers applying for positions in the state of Ohio per ORC 4796 either due to not meeting needed qualifications or did not get appointed within 2 years (OAC 109:2-1-12). Records include, but are not limited to, the application request for reciprocal occupational certificate, employment verification and other human resources documents, officer basic training certifications, webcheck background results, national decertification database results, reciprocal certificate checklist, and correspondence determining approval or rejection of application. Approved officer reciprocity applications will be retained with the peace officer's file under its own applicable records retention schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years after decision to reject or officer is not appointed within 2 years. ; Paper: Retain 3 years after decision to reject or officer is not appointed within 2 years. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately.

series_description: Documents support requests from users of the Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway (OHLEG) network by the Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) concerning such matters as how to use the OHLEG system, log in issues, or permission issues. Records include, but are not limited to, email requests and phone call records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 8 years from completion of request.

series_description: Per
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years from close of investigation. If complaint determined unsubstantial, retain 6 months. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately. ; Electronic: Retain 2 years from close of investigation. If complaint determined unsubstantial, retain 6 months.

series_description: Per
retention_statement: Paper: Retain inspection reports for 5 years. Any supporting documentation can be destroyed after 1 year. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately. ; Electronic: Retain inspection reports for 5 years. Any supporting documentation can be destroyed after 1 year.

series_description: Court orders received by the Ohio Attorney General's Office (AGO) concerning lawsuits the AGO is not involved in, but is required to provide documents, testimony, or other information relevant to the case. Records include, but are not limited to, the subpoena received, correspondence, and responsive documents sent by the AGO. If the AGO files a motion to quash and litigation commences, those records would be retained under the applicable court case files records retention schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 year from completion of legal mandate. ; Paper: Retain 1 year from completion of legal mandate. If scanned and saved electronically, dispose of paper immediately.

series_description: Documents training certificates completed by employees of the Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI), kept solely by BCI staff, for purposes of supporting work functions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until employee separates from the office.
